sources.title: Microsoft Cloud and AI Strength Fuels Third Quarter Results
sources.evidence_summary: Microsoft reported FY26 Q3 revenue of $82.9 billion, operating income of $38.4 billion, net income of $31.8 billion, diluted EPS of $4.27, and Microsoft Cloud revenue of $54.5 billion. The release states that forward-looking guidance would be provided on the earnings call and webcast.
limitations: Company source; validates reported facts and official management context, not independent valuation judgment.

sources.title: Microsoft Form 10-Q for Quarter Ended March 31, 2026
sources.evidence_summary: SEC-hosted 10-Q provides official financial statements and notes for Microsoft for the quarter ended March 31, 2026.
limitations: Filing supports historical and regulatory facts; forward guidance still requires earnings-call or issuer guidance evidence.

sources.title: Microsoft (MSFT) Q3 2026 Earnings Transcript
sources.evidence_summary: Transcript coverage includes management forward-looking commentary, including Q4 cost and operating-expense guidance and fiscal 2026 operating-margin commentary. Use as secondary transcript support unless an issuer transcript is directly available.
limitations: Secondary transcript; prefer issuer-hosted transcript if available.

sources.title: Microsoft Q3 2026 Earnings Report
sources.evidence_summary: MarketBeat summarizes Microsoft Q3 FY2026 results, management guidance, Q4 revenue guidance near $86.7 billion to $87.8 billion, and AI-capex context. Use as secondary estimate and guidance cross-check.
limitations: Secondary aggregator; does not replace issuer guidance or SEC filing evidence.

sources.title: Microsoft (MSFT) Earnings Date and Reports 2026
sources.evidence_summary: MarketBeat lists Microsoft earnings history and estimate context, including Q3 FY2026 EPS of $4.27 versus consensus of $4.04. Use as a secondary catalyst-calendar and estimates cross-check.
supported_claims: Confirmed catalyst calendar and next earnings date
limitations: Secondary earnings-calendar source; issuer IR confirmation is preferred when available.

sources.title: Microsoft (MSFT) Stock Forecast and Analyst Price Targets
sources.evidence_summary: StockAnalysis reports analyst forecast context for MSFT, including a Strong Buy consensus, 36 analysts, and an average price target near $565.75 as of the source page.
limitations: Secondary forecast aggregator; values should be reconciled against current price and other providers.

sources.title: Microsoft (MSFT) Stock Forecast and Price Target
sources.evidence_summary: MarketBeat reports analyst consensus context for Microsoft, including 44 analyst ratings, a Moderate Buy consensus, and an average price target near $556.15.
limitations: Secondary analyst aggregator; does not replace direct analyst notes or primary evidence.

sources.title: Microsoft (MSFT) Short Interest and Short Float
sources.evidence_summary: MarketBeat short-interest coverage reports March 31, 2026 short interest of 80,991,357 shares, about 1.09% of shares, and a short-interest ratio near 2.3 days. Use as a secondary positioning cross-check unless replaced by direct exchange/FINRA data.
limitations: Secondary short-interest source; direct exchange or FINRA source remains preferred for production-grade validation.

sources.title: Everyone has an opinion on Microsoft's spending, but the real story is what the numbers say about its position
sources.evidence_summary: Industry coverage discusses Microsoft's FY26 Q3 cloud and AI results and investor focus on AI capital spending.
limitations: Industry news; use as context, not primary financial-statement evidence.

sources.title: Federal Reserve FOMC Statement - April 2026
sources.evidence_summary: Official FOMC calendar/statement source for late-April policy context and rate-path interpretation.

claim_links.rationale: Microsoft issuer evidence confirms the FY26 Q3 result set and states that forward-looking guidance was provided on the call. Secondary transcript and MarketBeat sources provide the guidance context needed for downstream synthesis, with issuer-transcript preference preserved as a residual caveat.
unresolved_requirements: Prefer issuer-hosted transcript or official guidance table if available.

claim_links.rationale: A secondary earnings-calendar source provides date and estimate context. Treat future event dates as catalysts only when source date is as-of valid; preserve issuer-confirmation caveat.
unresolved_requirements: Retrieve issuer IR event calendar or exchange calendar confirmation for the next earnings date.

claim_links.rationale: Secondary short-interest and analyst-consensus sources provide numeric short-interest and consensus context. Crowding or squeeze claims must not be inferred from tone alone.
unresolved_requirements: Prefer direct exchange/FINRA short-interest data in production.

claim_links.rationale: The baseline stance is preserved as model output but must be validated through price, fundamental, citation, graph, and horizon checks.
unresolved_requirements: Final stance requires downstream Titan horizon validation.

claim_links.rationale: Forward valuation can be strengthened by direct calculation from report price and externally sourced EPS/forward multiple inputs.
unresolved_requirements: Run Stage 2C metric reconciliation before final valuation language.

claim_links.rationale: Official policy context and industry reporting support macro/sector context. This remains market backdrop, not direct issuer validation.
Stage 1 and SEC EDGAR evidence provide official regulatory support for core issuer facts.
